title: Tailwind v4 duration-token namespace mismatch (--duration-* vs --transition-duration-*)
description:
T03 ported --duration-fast/--duration-base into globals.css as plain CSS vars, but Tailwind v4's duration-* utilities are keyed off the --transition-duration-* namespace. As a result every duration-fast/duration-base class app-wide (button.tsx, switch.tsx, mobile-nav, possibly more) was silently emitting no utility and falling back to the default 150ms — the design's base(220ms)/fast(150ms) motion tokens were never actually applied as utilities.

The sidebar-fix agent worked around it locally (mobile-nav uses arbitrary [transition-duration:var(--dur-base)]), but the systemic fix is a one-line rename in globals.css: --duration-fast → --transition-duration-fast, --duration-base → --transition-duration-base (verify the exact Tailwind v4 namespace against the installed version via context7 first).

Verify against: docs/design_prototype (motion tokens), web/app/globals.css:348-350, and grep all duration-fast/duration-base usages. After the rename, audit that no transition silently changed timing in a regressions-introducing way (some may have been accidentally-correct at 150ms and should explicitly become duration-fast).

Investigate the CURRENT sidebar (web/components/shell/app-side-nav.tsx + sidebar-nav.tsx + nav-config.ts) against docs/design_prototype/SideNav.dc.html + the standalone bundle — RENDER it (pnpm dev) and compare structure + visual detail (brand lockup, labeled Mode control, nav group headers, row spacing/rhythm, step badges + counts, full-row active/hover treatment, account/theme footer). Honor authoritative constraints (NOT divergences): DL10 (no role field; no Roster/AI in committed nav), DL06/FR-ST-28 (committed labels People/Shift Types, not Staff/Shifts), all-capabilities-reachable-in-Guided. Enumerate the specific divergences ranked by severity with prototype-ref vs impl file:line + concrete fix, then a focused fix pass on Claude + codex cold review. Separate from the in-flight T13 rebuild.
close_reason: Sidebar prototype-conformance fix complete and cold-reviewed CLEAN (0/0/0/0, codex/terra). All 6 Major + 2 Minor from the audit closed: DL10-D5 nav taxonomy (Home headerless + SET UP/OUTPUT/SYSTEM), trailing step cluster, 500/600 weights + 42px rows, prototype icon vocabulary, single 34px footer theme button (DisplaySettings deleted), 250px mobile drawer, role=tablist/tab with full roving-keyboard contract, scrim/motion. 85/85 Playwright, 474/17 serial vitest, gates green. T08 (P0 shell rebuild) remains closed; this bead was the follow-up sidebar-conformance remediation.

title: T03 token collision: --on-ink == --chrome in dark theme (#0f1319)
description: During T08 cold-review fixes, the implementer found that in dark theme the design-system tokens --on-ink and --chrome are both #0f1319, so text-on-ink on a chrome/ink background renders invisible (1:1 contrast) in dark mode. T08 worked around it locally by using text-onbrand (#fff in both themes) for chrome controls, but the underlying token collision remains and will bite any chrome surface using text-on-ink in dark theme (incl. downstream editors T09-T15). Fix: give --on-ink a value in dark theme that actually contrasts with --chrome, or clarify the intended semantics of on-ink vs onbrand for chrome surfaces. Verify in web/app/globals.css.
close_reason: Fixed: .dark --on-ink #0f1319 -> #ffffff (no longer inverts, since ink/chrome bar is dark in both themes). Retired T08's onbrand workaround — chrome surfaces (top-bar, undo/redo, mobile-nav trigger) now use the correct text-on-ink token. Verified: typecheck 0, vitest 302 passed, prod build 17/17, oxlint/oxfmt clean; contrast-neutral vs prior onbrand (#fff both themes).

title: Ask how to set cors allowed domain ?
description: can we set it via env ? since env already sets the api domain
notes:
RESOLVED via the BFF architecture (DL11 D1 / tech-plan §3, ticket T06).

Q: can we set the CORS allowed domain via env, since env already sets the api domain?

A: You don't need to. Two parts:
1. NORMAL OPERATION — no CORS config needed. With the Next same-origin BFF, the browser only ever calls /api/* on the Next origin; the Next server calls the backend server-side inside the Docker network. The browser therefore NEVER triggers the backend's CORS allow-list, so the backend CORS domain is irrelevant to the deployed app. cloudflared fronts 'web'; 'web'->'backend' is server-side.
2. TODAY IT IS NOT ENV-DRIVEN — the backend CORS is a hardcoded regex in serve.py (allow_origin_regex = localhost | *.nursescheduling.org, C2 §Conformance). It is not read from env. The API domain the frontend uses is env (BACKEND_API_URL, server-side) — a different thing from the backend's CORS allow-list.
3. IF direct browser->backend access is ever wanted (bypassing the BFF) from a custom domain, making the CORS origin env-configurable would be a small backend config adaptation (same spirit as DL11 D2's version-fn change) — out of scope now; not required for the BFF deploy.
close_reason: Answered: BFF makes backend CORS config unnecessary for normal operation (same-origin); backend CORS is a hardcoded regex, not env — env only sets the server-side BACKEND_API_URL. See notes + DL11 D1 / T06.

title: Backend: roster-data endpoint for in-app roster viewer
description: New backend contract required by the in-app roster viewer. Today serve.py/C2 returns only the styled XLSX blob + score/status; there is no endpoint that returns structured, renderable roster data (per person x date assignments). Design and ship an endpoint that returns the solved roster as structured data so the frontend can render/adjust it. This blocks the frontend viewer task.
acceptance_criteria: An HTTP endpoint returns the solved roster as structured per-(person,date) assignment data (not just the XLSX blob); documented as a new C2 contract addition.

title: Frontend: in-app roster viewer (render + adjust)
description: Build the real in-app roster viewer (prototype reference: ScreenSchedule — grid/day/coverage views, tap-to-edit, drag-swap, warnings). Renders from the new backend roster-data endpoint (NOT fabricated data as in the prototype). Depends on the backend roster-data endpoint task.
acceptance_criteria: The solved roster renders in-app from real backend data after an optimize run; edits/warnings behave per the design; XLSX download path unchanged.

title: Post-parity: In-app roster viewer (built for real)
description: Beyond-parity initiative from DL10 (design-review product decision D3). The design prototype includes an exploratory in-app roster viewer (ScreenSchedule) that lets a user view/adjust the solved roster in-app. It is NOT part of the parity rebuild: today the backend returns only the opaque XLSX blob + score/status (contract C2) and has no endpoint that returns structured roster data for rendering. This initiative builds the roster viewer for real. Higher priority than the AI features initiative; both are tracked separately from the parity rebuild and must not block it.
design: Requires a NEW backend contract (roster-data endpoint) designed and shipped BEFORE the frontend viewer can be built. Sequence: backend endpoint -> frontend viewer. Keep the existing XLSX-download path intact as the parity contract; the roster viewer is additive.
acceptance_criteria: A user can view the solved roster in-app (rendered from real backend roster data, not fabricated), after the optimize run completes; the XLSX download path is unchanged.

title: De-flake instrumentation.subprocess.test.ts under default Vitest parallelism (ephemeral ports)
description: instrumentation.subprocess.test.ts uses hardcoded fixed ports (34571/34572) and the valid-config test couples PORT to PUBLIC_ORIGIN (http://localhost:34572). Under Vitest DEFAULT parallelism this flakes via port contention (passes serial / --no-file-parallelism). Multiple review rounds confirmed the flake is confined to THIS non-T09 test. Fix: introduce an ephemeral/randomized-port helper that keeps PORT and PUBLIC_ORIGIN in sync so the subprocess tests are isolation-safe under parallelism. Verify: passes under default parallel run repeatedly; fail-fast behavior + assertions unchanged. Split out from nursing-sheduler-cfh to keep that a clean single-concern instrumentation-warning edit.

title: Fix pre-existing Turbopack warning in web/instrumentation.ts
description:
Pre-existing Turbopack warning emitted for web/instrumentation.ts (flagged by user 2026-07-17). Track + fix as a focused pass.

STEP 1 — capture the EXACT warning text/code first (do NOT assume the cause): run a clean Turbopack build/dev and record the verbatim warning + which line it points at. Suspected (UNVERIFIED) trigger: the dynamic `await import('@/lib/backend')` inside register() at instrumentation.ts:16, or a Turbopack instrumentation-hook pattern constraint in Next 16. Verify against Next 16 / Turbopack official docs (context7) before changing anything — do not guess.

STEP 2 — fix so the warning is gone WITHOUT regressing the fail-fast behavior that instrumentation.ts deliberately implements: register() must still validate BFF config on Node server boot and process.exit(1) BEFORE the port binds on invalid config (see the file's header comment + instrumentation.test.ts + instrumentation.subprocess.test.ts). Keep NEXT_RUNTIME==='nodejs' guard and the build-phase skip.

CONTEXT / related: instrumentation.subprocess.test.ts 'standalone server config fail-fast' is a fixed-port test that flakes under Vitest default parallelism (passes serial). That flake is a SEPARATE test-isolation issue (fixed port contention), not the Turbopack warning — but it lives in the same area; consider whether a fix should also de-flake it (e.g. ephemeral/randomized port) or leave it to a separate ticket.

Timing: do NOT run a concurrent next build while the T08 rebuild (nursing-sheduler-qq0.8) is in flight — schedule after T08 lands, or in an isolated worktree. Verify: clean build shows NO instrumentation warning; instrumentation.test.ts + instrumentation.subprocess.test.ts (serial) still pass; typecheck/oxlint/oxfmt clean.
close_reason: Fixed the Turbopack Edge warning: process.exit at instrumentation.ts:22 was statically compiled into the Edge bundle. Doc-confirmed idiomatic fix — extracted the Node-only fail-fast into new web/instrumentation-node.ts, loaded via dynamic import only under NEXT_RUNTIME==='nodejs'. Applied+verified on main: build 0 warnings (worktree), focused instrumentation vitest 5 pass/1 skip, typecheck 0, oxlint+oxfmt clean. Fail-fast (process.exit(1) before port bind, build-phase skip, nodejs guard) preserved. Subprocess-flake follow-up filed separately.

title: Consider: bare-duration (hours-only) shift authoring for contracted-hours accounting
description:
OPEN QUESTION / think-about item surfaced during T09 (People & Shift Types editors) cold re-review Major 7.

DL10-D4 settled that T09's working-time editor is clock-<select>-only (start/end + rest → auto-derived read-only duration); the design prototype overrides spec 03 FR-ED-06a for authoring, and T09 will NOT expose a bare-duration authoring mode. That is correct for ordinary FLOOR shifts, where the clock in/out + rest fully define the shift and the duration is a consequence.

BUT the core validator + producer schema DO accept a valid bare '{durationMinutes: N}' shape (clocks absent). The one place that shape is genuinely meaningful is HOURS-ONLY accounting toward contracted hours — e.g. a flexible/training/on-call block that contributes N hours toward a person's contracted-hours target without a fixed clock window. This is a T12 (Card editors + Contracted Hours, nursing-sheduler-qq0.12) / DL09 (minute-unit + working-time coefficients) concern, NOT a floor-shift working-time concern.

THINK ABOUT (defer decision to T12): Does the contracted-hours model need users to author duration-only shift types (no clocks)? If yes, where does that authoring live — a dedicated contracted-hours/coefficient surface (T12), NOT the T09 floor-shift working-time editor? If such shapes can arise from import/backend, T09 already preserves them on unrelated edits (DL10-D4 data-preservation guardrail) — confirm that guardrail is sufficient vs. needing real authoring.

No action now. Revisit when T12 is planned/implemented. Cross-refs: DL10-D4 (decision-logs/10-design-review-product-decisions), T09 cold-review Major 7, spec 03 FR-ED-06a, spec 12 contracted-hours, DL09.

title: Post-Wave-A: DRY Dates (T10) date-group editor onto T09 shared entity-editor
description: Spec 03 mandates a shared ItemGroupEditorPage across People/Shift-Types/Dates. T09 owns and builds the generic shared editor at web/components/entity-editor/** (domain-agnostic via a descriptor interface). Because T10 (Dates) runs in parallel in Wave A, it cannot consume T09's mid-build module, so T10 builds its date-group create/rename UI self-contained in components/dates/** for now. Once both T09 and T10 land, refactor T10's date-group management to consume T09's shared entity-editor via a Dates domain descriptor, removing the duplication. Verify no behavior change + cascade/serialization still correct.
close_reason: T10 Dates & Calendar REBUILT to the design prototype (ScreenDates): two-column work area, roster-period card + Date-IDs explainer, prototype calendar state language (Monday-first, in-range band, solid START/END endpoints, holiday markers+reactive title/aria, 3-item legend), group create + inline-edit cards, derived-group selectable PREVIEW chips (immutable). fs7 folded in: date-group editor routes through the shared entity-editor CORE via a datesDescriptor with a producer/T07-parity date-literal ID validator. Shared DateScopePicker extracted (clean controlled API) for T13/T12 reuse. Dead reduced-surface code deleted. Correctness preserved (WORKDAY collision, UTC, SG holidays, one-zundo). Cold review clean 0/0/0/0 across prototype-fidelity + dead-code + correctness + shared-primitive; gates green (typecheck 0, vitest 474/17, playwright 68/68 incl 14 Dates, extreme-TZ 44/44).

title: Future: configurable / multi-type leave model (leave-configuration vision)
description:
Beyond-parity FUTURE vision, previously only a spec-level [DEFERRED] fence (spec 12 + DL05) — now tracked as a bead so it surfaces in bd. Currently NOT built; parity ships the single fixed-8h LEAVE day-state only. The vision, if pursued later:
- A top-level 'leaveTypes' container in the scenario schema + a backend 'creditMinutes' store (the backend today has NEITHER and extra="forbid" REJECTS leaveTypes — so this needs backend/contract work, not just UI).
- Configurable leave credit instead of the fixed 8h (LEAVE_CREDIT_MINUTES=480 frontend constant).
- Multiple / half-day leave types (e.g. Full-Day 8h + Half-Day 4h) + a Leave Types editor UI surface.
- Unit-toggle integer-safety for UNMARKED/generic shift counts (FR-CH-33 reconciliation) + hours semantics for unmarked generic counts.
Trigger to un-defer: a product decision to expand the leave model beyond the single fixed-8h type. Not calendar-driven.
design: Requires a NEW backend contract (leaveTypes container + creditMinutes store) BEFORE the frontend Leave Types editor is meaningful; backend currently rejects leaveTypes. Independent of beads cjr (roster) and t34 (AI). Rationale/fence lives in spec 12 (12-contracted-hours-and-leave) and DL05 (decision-logs/05-contracted-hours-and-leave-model). Kept as spec fences (NOT beaded): telemetry/ops non-goals, backlog nudges (raw-YAML guard channel, discovery nudge, bulk leave-calendar import, on-screen hours report), and t01 Home/App-Shell.
acceptance_criteria: A user can define multiple/configurable leave types (credit + optionally half-day) through a Leave Types editor, round-tripped through a backend that accepts leaveTypes; the fixed single-8h behavior remains the default.

title: Mobile person×date matrix — density strategy + touch paint (accepted-suboptimal, deferred)
description:
Design-completeness gap from the parity/feasibility review (findings 1-2). DECISION (2026-07-15): proceed with the current non-optimal design for the parity build; defer the improvement. Two issues on the Shift Requests person×date matrix — the handover's flagged KEY mobile challenge (handover section 8):

(1) Density = horizontal-scroll-only. The matrix answers ~100 people × ~31 days with a nested overflow:auto pane + sticky first column (shrink-a-table), not the progressive-disclosure / focused-row strategy the handover asked for. On a phone the 150px sticky nurse column eats ~40% of the viewport (~5-6 day cells visible); body scrolls ~3400px inside a 68vh pane; nested scroll fights page scroll. (ScreenRequests 90,94,523,536.)

(2) Quick-paint bulk entry is mouse-only (mousedown/mouseenter/global mouseup; no touch/pointer events) — the bulk-fill gesture is dead on touch, the priority device class. Single-cell tap-to-edit still works, so it degrades to one-cell-at-a-time. (ScreenRequests 347-350, 531-532.)

Revisit trigger: after the parity build ships / before any real mobile launch. Likely needs a design-track loop for a genuine mobile strategy (focused-row/progressive-disclosure view + touch-capable paint), not a mechanical patch.
design: Not a build-time patch — wants a real mobile matrix strategy from the design track (focused-row / progressive-disclosure view, virtualization, date-window paging) plus touch/pointer-event paint. Parity build proceeds with scroll-only + mouse paint as an accepted known limitation.
acceptance_criteria: The person×date matrix is usable on a phone for ~100 people × a month: bulk entry works on touch, and density is handled by a deliberate mobile strategy rather than horizontal scroll alone.

title: Post-parity: AI features (assist / conflict-fix)
description: Beyond-parity initiative from DL10 (design-review product decision D3). The design prototype includes an exploratory AI appendix (ScreenAppendixAI) plus an AI 'Planned' teaser that was removed from the committed Optimize flow. AI assist (e.g. reading an infeasible result and proposing targeted constraint fixes) has NO backend equivalent today and is out of the parity inventory (handover section 10). Build AFTER the roster-viewer initiative (roster takes higher priority). Tracked separately from the parity rebuild; must not block it.
design: No backend equivalent exists; needs its own backend/LLM contract designed first. Sequenced after the in-app roster initiative per the user's stated priority.
acceptance_criteria: AI assist is available as a clearly-separated capability wired to a real backend, not fabricated; it is not required by any committed parity flow.
